highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.144 Released
highlights.summary: The latest release adds `/resume` support for background sessions, so sessions started with `claude --bg` now appear alongside interactive ones in your session list. You can also see how long a background subagent ran once it completes, with elapsed time shown in notifications (e.g. '3h 2m 5s'). This makes managing long-running background tasks much easier to track.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.142 Released
highlights.summary: This release significantly expands the `claude agents` command with new flags like `--model`, `--effort`, `--permission-mode`, and `--mcp-config`, giving you fine-grained control over how dispatched background sessions behave. Fast mode now defaults to Opus 4.7 instead of 4.6, bringing the latest model improvements automatically. If you use background agents for long tasks, these new flags are worth exploring.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has officially announced Claude Opus 4.7, the newest model in the Opus family which is now the default for Fast mode in Claude Code. This release brings improved reasoning and coding capabilities relevant to developers using Claude Code daily. Upgrading to the latest Claude Code version will automatically put you on Opus 4.7 for Fast mode tasks.

highlights.title: How Claude Code Works in Large Codebases
highlights.summary: This Anthropic blog post explains best practices for using Claude Code when working on large, complex codebases — including where to start and how to guide Claude effectively. It covers strategies like breaking work into scoped tasks and leveraging project context so Claude doesn't get lost. Essential reading if your codebase has grown beyond a few hundred files.

highlights.title: Claude Code - Full Tutorial for Beginners
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial provides a comprehensive introduction to Claude Code for developers who are just getting started. It walks through installation, basic commands, and how to integrate Claude Code into your day-to-day development workflow. A great first watch if you've just signed up and aren't sure where to begin.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This video aims to take you from basic Claude Code usage to more advanced techniques in a single 30-minute session, making it efficient for developers with some prior exposure. Topics likely include prompt crafting, session management, and integrating Claude Code into real projects. A solid choice if beginner tutorials feel too slow but you haven't explored power-user features yet.

highlights.title: Session Amnesia: The Hidden Cost of Stateless AI Coding Assistants
highlights.summary: This article highlights how stateless AI coding tools lose all context between sessions, forcing developers to repeatedly re-explain their codebase and slowing down productivity. Understanding this limitation helps you design better workflows — for example, using CLAUDE.md files in Claude Code to persist project context across sessions. A useful read for anyone frustrated by repetitive onboarding of their AI assistant.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.143 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds smart plugin dependency enforcement: if you try to disable a plugin that another enabled plugin depends on, Claude Code will now warn you and provide a copy-pasteable command to disable the whole chain safely. It also introduces projected context cost estimates per turn, helping you monitor and control API spending more proactively. Power users managing multiple plugins will find both features immediately useful.

tips_of_the_day: Use a CLAUDE.md file at the root of your project to store key context (architecture overview, conventions, and goals) — this acts as persistent memory across sessions and reduces the 'explain your codebase from scratch' tax every time you start Claude Code.

When running long background tasks with `claude --bg`, upgrade to v2.1.144+ so you can use `/resume` to check in on those sessions and see elapsed time in completion notifications — no more guessing whether your overnight agent job finished.

Before disabling a plugin with `claude plugin disable`, run the command in v2.1.143+ to get a dependency warning and a ready-to-copy disable chain — this prevents silent breakage of plugins that rely on the one you're removing.
